title: Racosperma divergens
intro: Racosperma divergens, also known as the Darling Racosperma, is a shrub native to Australia. The Darling Racosperma has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Racosperma divergens include tea, tincture, and topical ointments, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
